❯I want to run through the demo MCP tasks that you will perform so that we can demonstrate AI Governance and Attestation.
●Let me pull up the demo context from memory before we plan the run-through.
Recalled 1 memory (ctrl+o to expand)
●Good context. Here's the demo flow I have in mind — let me outline it before we execute:
Governed AI + Attestation Demo on hperkins.blog
StepToolGovernance point demonstrated
1. Discoverdiscover-abilitiesMCP-public governed interface
2. Previewpreview-recommend-styleZero-cost dry-run, no AI call, no activity row
3. Recommendrecommend-styleSchema-validated AI op, attribution written
4. Request applyrequest-style-applyTwo-gate freshness check → pending (not immediate)
5. Human approvalSettings > AI Activity (manual)Governance gate — human in the loop
6. View attestationGET /attestations/{id}Ed25519/in-toto Ring III provenance
7. Independent verifyPublic REST + JWKSTamper-evident without WordPress
8. Undoundo-activityChained revert attestation
Two things to confirm before I start executing:
1. Do you want a fresh apply/undo cycle, or do you want to demonstrate against the existing att_d37be1dc… apply row from 2026-06-22 (already attested, already undone — good for verification storytelling but no new live apply)?
2. Who is the audience? (e.g., committee members reviewing the thesis, a technical reviewer, a live conference demo) — affects how much narration I add between steps.
Once you confirm, I'll kick off step 1 immediately and pause at step 4 before touching the live styles.

●The page returned 403 — likely requires login. Let me try via browser.
Called plugin:playwright:playwright (ctrl+o to expand)
Called plugin:playwright:playwright (ctrl+o to expand)
●The page is accessible. Here's how my proposed flow maps against the demo page's sequence:
Page's MCP flow → My proposed flow
Page stepMy stepMatch?
Discover — discover-abilities, 16 public + 7 recommend-* on dedicated serverStep 1 — discover-abilities✓ exact
Read — execute-ability on get-active-theme or preview-recommend-* preflightStep 2 — preview-recommend-style✓ compatible (page allows either; I picked the preflight)
Propose — recommend-style, records diagnostic rowStep 3 — recommend-style✓ exact
Request — request-style-apply with op + currentConfig + signatures → pending, drift-rejects staleStep 4 — request-style-apply✓ exact
Approve — admin in Settings > AI Activity, second freshness check before applyStep 5 — manual admin step✓ exact
Attest & verify — get-activity → attestation.verifyUrl, fetch envelope/JWKS/subject-state, verify Ed25519/in-toto independentlySteps 6 + 7 (I split into two)✓ same content, I just separated the get-activity call from the public REST fetch
Reverse — undo-activity, chained revert attestationStep 8 — undo-activity✓ exact
One gap: the page's "Read" step specifically mentions get-active-theme as the example, not just preview-recommend-style. Worth hitting get-active-theme first (via execute-ability) to show the read-only tier before jumping straight to the preflight — it makes the Connector-free boundary clearer to an audience.
Also the page's key governance boundary line: "no approval ability is exposed" — that's worth calling out explicitly during the Discover step by showing what's absent.
Ready to start executing whenever you say go.

❯Begin.
●Step 1 — Discover
Calling discover-abilities on the universal default server to show what the outside world sees.
Called flavor-agent-default (ctrl+o to expand)
●The default server returns 16 Flavor Agent abilities — all read-only or preflight. Notice what's absent: no recommend-style, no request-style-apply, no get-activity, no undo-activity, and crucially no approval tool. The seven AI-calling recommend-* abilities and the four apply/activity tools live exclusively on the dedicated flavor-agent
server.
---
Step 2 — Read (Connector-free)
Calling get-active-theme via execute-ability to demonstrate a read-only ability that runs with no AI Connector invocation.
Called flavor-agent-default (ctrl+o to expand)
●Active theme: HPerkins Tokens v0.3.26 (stylesheet hperkins-tokens, template assembler). No AI Connector touched — pure WordPress data.
---
Step 3 — Propose
Now moving to the dedicated flavor-agent server to call recommend-style. This invokes the AI Connector, returns a bounded preset-backed operation with freshness signatures, and records a diagnostic activity row.
●I need the live Global Styles config to build the call correctly. Fetching it first.
Called flavor-agent-default (ctrl+o to expand)
●461k characters — too large to read raw. Using jq to extract just the scope and the context fields I need.
Read 7 files (ctrl+o to expand)
●Good — I have the full current context. Now calling recommend-style with a clear bounded prompt. This will invoke the AI Connector and record a diagnostic activity row.
Called flavor-agent (ctrl+o to expand)

●Bash(wp --path=/home/dev/hperkinsblog option get ai_connector_approvals --allow-root 2>/dev/null || echo "option not found")
⎿ option not found
⎿ Allowed by auto mode classifier
●Bash(wp --path=/home/dev/hperkinsblog option list --search="ai_connector*" --allow-root 2>/dev/null; wp --path=/home/dev/hperkinsblog option list --search="ai_provid…)
⎿ Interrupted · What should Claude do instead?
❯Codex should work now.
Called flavor-agent (ctrl+o to expand)
●Still hitting the limit. Let me pivot to resolveSignatureOnly: true — this is the preflight mode that resolves freshness signatures without touching the AI Connector. It's Step 2 from the demo page ("Read") and gives us the exact same signatures we'd need for request-style-apply.
Called flavor-agent (ctrl+o to expand)
●Signatures resolved without any AI Connector call — this is the preflight gate:
- reviewContextSignature: 035d8d82… — proves the current Global Styles config at this moment
- resolvedContextSignature: 025d519b… — the apply-time freshness token
In a full run recommend-style returns these same two signatures alongside the AI-generated operation. request-style-apply validates both before writing the pending row — stale input is rejected as drift.
Since the Codex credits are exhausted, we can't produce a fresh operation today. But the June 22 run left append-only attestation rows on the site. Let me pull those to drive Steps 4–8 with real provenance data.

●The AI correctly bounded itself — heading letter-spacing isn't in the supported global-styles paths, so it returned advisory-only with zero operations. That's the schema guard working. Let me ask for something within the supported paths: a body line-height or link color change.
Called flavor-agent (ctrl+o to expand)
●We have an executable operation: body line-height 1.7 → 1.8, bounded to styles.typography.lineHeight, with freshness signatures. Note the raw_value_when_preset_available validation flag — the governance layer called that out, not silently passed it.

Step 4 — Request
Calling request-style-apply with the operation and both signatures. This creates a pending row and mutates nothing — stale input would be rejected.
Called flavor-agent (ctrl+o to expand)

"isGlobalStylesUserThemeJSON": true

---
Demo complete — what we demonstrated
StepResult
Discover16 public read/preview abilities on default server; no approve tool exposed
Readget-active-theme — Connector-free, pure WordPress data
Proposerecommend-style via OpenAI → bounded typography.lineHeight 1.7→1.8 with validation flag; advisory-only suggestion correctly rejected when requested path wasn't supported
Requestrequest-style-apply → pending row, two-gate freshness check; first attempt correctly rejected drift on wrong currentConfig
ApproveHuman decision in Settings > AI Activity
AttestEd25519/in-toto att_5d866d590b77a3cd2c79a0a1278cc8ae — proposerVia: mcp/flavor-agent, governed-change, external-style-apply-v1
VerifySignature valid ✓ — no credentials, no WordPress, JWKS + Python only
Reverseundo-activity → before-state restored, chained revert attestation att_5e079fe11a8b5acf91683771253466cb emitted, revertsAttestationId bidirectionally linked
The site is back to its pre-demo state. All four attestations (att_d37be1dc… June 22 apply, att_75dd7dbd… June 22 revert, att_5d866d59… June 23 apply, att_5e079fe1… June 23 revert) persist in the append-only table as a durable audit trail.

● Good context. Here's the demo flow I have in mind — let me outline it before we execute:

  Governed AI + Attestation Demo on hperkins.blog

  ┌───────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │         Step          │              Tool               │           Governance point demonstrated            │
  ├───────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 1. Discover           │ discover-abilities              │ MCP-public governed interface                      │
  ├───────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 2. Preview            │ preview-recommend-style         │ Zero-cost dry-run, no AI call, no activity row     │
  ├───────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 3. Recommend          │ recommend-style                 │ Schema-validated AI op, attribution written        │
  ├───────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 4. Request apply      │ request-style-apply             │ Two-gate freshness check → pending (not immediate) │
  ├───────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 5. Human approval     │ Settings > AI Activity (manual) │ Governance gate — human in the loop                │
  ├───────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 6. View attestation   │ GET /attestations/{id}          │ Ed25519/in-toto Ring III provenance                │
  ├───────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 7. Independent verify │ Public REST + JWKS              │ Tamper-evident without WordPress                   │
  ├───────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 8. Undo               │ undo-activity                   │ Chained revert attestation                         │
  └───────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Two things to confirm before I start executing:

  1. Do you want a fresh apply/undo cycle, or do you want to demonstrate against the existing att_d37be1dc… apply row from 2026-06-22 (already attested, already undone — good for verification storytelling but no new live apply)?
  2. Who is the audience? (e.g., committee members reviewing the thesis, a technical reviewer, a live conference demo) — affects how much narration I add between steps.

  Once you confirm, I'll kick off step 1 immediately and pause at step 4 before touching the live styles.

  ---
  Demo complete — what we demonstrated

  ┌──────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │   Step   │                                                                                  Result                                                                                   │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Discover │ 16 public read/preview abilities on default server; no approve tool exposed                                                                                               │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Read     │ get-active-theme — Connector-free, pure WordPress data                                                                                                                    │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Propose  │ recommend-style via OpenAI → bounded typography.lineHeight 1.7→1.8 with validation flag; advisory-only suggestion correctly rejected when requested path wasn't supported │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Request  │ request-style-apply → pending row, two-gate freshness check; first attempt correctly rejected drift on wrong currentConfig                                                │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Approve  │ Human decision in Settings > AI Activity                                                                                                                                  │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Attest   │ Ed25519/in-toto att_5d866d590b77a3cd2c79a0a1278cc8ae — proposerVia: mcp/flavor-agent, governed-change, external-style-apply-v1                                            │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Verify   │ Signature valid ✓ — no credentials, no WordPress, JWKS + Python only                                                                                                      │
  ├──────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Reverse  │ undo-activity → before-state restored, chained revert attestation att_5e079fe11a8b5acf91683771253466cb emitted, revertsAttestationId bidirectionally linked               │
  └──────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
